fields.label: Hours Per Week
fields.help: Number of study hours per week.

explanation:
**Study methods and learning approaches** are grounded in cognitive science research (Ebbinghaus, Bjork, Dunlosky). Techniques like spaced repetition, active recall, and interleaving have strong empirical support.

## How This Is Calculated

Estimated progression time between A1–C2 levels based on weekly study hours. This calculator applies the formula with your inputs and alerts you if values fall outside typical ranges.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide only. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are updated to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: A1 is Beginner level, typically requiring 100 hours of study to achieve.
A2 is Elementary level, approximately 200 hours of cumulative study.
B1 is Intermediate level, roughly 400 hours. You can handle everyday conversations.
B2 is Upper Intermediate level, about 700 hours. Sufficient for working in English environments.
C1 is Advanced level, 1000+ hours. Academic and professional proficiency.
C2 is Mastery level, 1500+ hours. Near-native fluency and precision.

faq.q: How do English exams map to CEFR levels?
faq.a: Cambridge FCE = B2, CAE = C1, CPE = C2. IELTS 6.5+ ≈ B2.
Yes, all our calculators are free and require no registration.

faq.q: Does study quality matter as much as hours?
faq.a: Absolutely. Active learning (spaced repetition, speaking practice) accelerates progress beyond raw hours.
